title: Programatically update automations & integrations on board using API
excerpt:
Hi guys, 
Is/will it be possible to ever get and mutate a boards automations or integrations in GraphQL? 
Thanks

posts.author: isaac.dunnett (Isaac Dunnett)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Is/will it be possible to ever get and mutate a boards automations or integrations in GraphQL?

Thanks

posts.author: Scott-monday.com (Scott Underwood)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @isaac.dunnett

As of now, it’s not currently possible to add or remove automations or integrations via the GraphQL API. While it certainly might be possible in the future, it’s not on our immediate roadmap and I don’t foresee it happening in the coming months.

With that said, you can manually add automations or integrations via the monday.com UI, and then utilize the API call to trigger them. So, for example, if an automation is triggered upon a specific Status Column change, if you generate that Status Column change via the API, it will then trigger the relevant automation

-Scott

posts.author: isaac.dunnett (Isaac Dunnett)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Scott-monday.com

Any update on this at this point in time?

posts.author: jmtornetta (Jon Tornetta)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also interested in this. Automations should be accessible via API. They are impossible to maintain at any modest scale if they do not support inheritance nor are callable via API.

posts.author: jleni (Juan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here… this is really a deal breaker for us…
we really need to configure the integration programatically.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone!

I do not have an ETA on this feature but I will make sure of adding your votes to it so that it hopefully gets moved up in the list

Cheers,
-Matias

posts.author: gabemocan (Gabriel Mocan)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is highly deserved. Currently is impossible to alter automations across all boards when talking about hundreds of boards.

Other solution would be inheritance, so we would only alter the template, cascading the changes to dependent boards.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @gabemocan and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

I have added your vote to this feature request!

Thank you for the feedback

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @EBashkoff!

We do not have any updates about this at the moment. Any changes regarding this will be anounced in our changelog

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bumping this because I’ve heard it brought up by other customers.

Please vote on this feature if you want to see it added to our roadmap! We use community votes to track how popular a request is and it’s key to prioritize between new features.

posts.author: divory (Dan Ivory)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this. It would be great to be able to offer this to our app users and save them some manual set up steps.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @divory,

Did you vote at the top of the page?

posts.author: ammaarsuldev (Ammaar Sultan)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this, we would love to be able to use an integration recipe triggered from a custom trigger as a core functionality of our app but requiring customers to manually install them is a deal breaker for us.

posts.author: thomfilg-clickpart (thompson filgueiras)
posts.cookedHtml: I also interested on it. Or in a way to fully duplicate a folder structure maintaining its automations on duplicated items. For each new company that is our client, we need to create a new folder, 1 document and 3 boards. Each board has some automations. The main problem is that when a given status is set we need change from pending board to progress and from progress to archived. It’s annoying to have to manually do this.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the vote and the feedback @thomfilg-clickpart !

posts.author: Scherebart (Bartłomiej)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, we do also have a case to at least automatically disable/enable an existing automation. Voted for this feature!
